description_long: This axe features a long, wooden handle with a natural brown finish, showing some carved or engraved markings along its length. The head of the axe is strikingly unique, with a white, semi-circular blade that has a spiked, gear-like edge, giving it a menacing and unconventional appearance. The blade is attached to the handle with visible metallic pins or bolts, and there are red stains and splatters on both the blade and handle, suggesting recent or heavy use. The rear of the axe head is flat and reinforced, possibly for hammering. The handle is slightly faceted, providing a better grip, and the overall construction appears robust and functional. This object stands out due to its combination of traditional materials and an unusual, almost improvised blade design.
description: A long-handled axe with a spiked, white semi-circular blade and red stains, featuring a wooden grip and metallic fastenings. The design is both functional and visually striking.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the spiked, gear-like white blade with red stains.
description_view_1: End-on view of the handle, highlighting carved markings and the narrow profile of the axe.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the blade's spiked edge and the attachment points to the handle.
description_view_3: Top-down view displaying the alignment of the blade, the metallic fastenings, and the red-stained handle surface.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight wooden handle with a faceted cross-section, carved markings, and a rough, grippy surface.
attribute_blade_design: Semi-circular, white blade with a spiked, gear-like edge and red stains; single-edged with a reinforced poll.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional mounting with visible metallic pins or bolts securing the blade to the handle.
